description: Implement authentication system with login/signup UI, session management, role-based access control, admin panel, and all required Supabase SQL in SUPABASE_SETUP.md. The app must degrade gracefully when auth is not configured.
steps: Step 1 - Create utils/auth.py with the following functions: (a) check_auth_configured() - returns True if SUPABASE_URL + SUPABASE_KEY are set AND a dp_users table is expected. (b) sign_up(email, password, display_name) - uses Supabase Auth to create a user, then inserts a row in dp_users with role='viewer' (unless email matches ADMIN_EMAIL secret, then role='admin'). (c) sign_in(email, password) - authenticates via Supabase Auth, fetches user role from dp_users, stores session in st.session_state (keys: dp_authenticated=True, dp_user_email, dp_user_id, dp_user_role, dp_user_name, dp_login_time). (d) sign_out() - clears auth session state keys. (e) get_current_user() - returns dict with user info from session_state or None. (f) require_auth() - checks if user is authenticated; if not, renders the login page and calls st.stop(). Returns user dict if authenticated. (g) require_role(min_role) - checks user has sufficient role (admin > analyst > viewer); shows error and stops if not. (h) log_user_activity(action, details='') - logs to dp_activity_log table with user_id, timestamp. (i) is_admin() - shortcut returning True if current user role is 'admin'. The module should import from utils/supabase_client.py and follow the (ok, payload) pattern. When Supabase is not configured, require_auth() should return a mock admin user and NOT block access (local dev mode).

Step 2 - Create the login/signup UI component inside utils/auth.py as a function render_login_page(). This renders: app logo/title, tab group with 'Sign In' and 'Sign Up' tabs, email/password fields, submit buttons, error/success messages. Style it to match the enterprise theme. The sign-up form should also have a 'Display Name' field.

Step 3 - Modify app.py to integrate authentication: After inject_global_css() and init_all_session_state(), add: from utils.auth import require_auth, get_current_user; user = require_auth(). If user is None, st.stop() is already called inside require_auth(). After auth passes, show user info in the sidebar (name, role, logout button). Keep all existing sidebar content below the user section.

Step 4 - Create pages/12_Admin_Panel.py with: (a) Page config and CSS injection. (b) require_role('admin') check at the top. (c) Tab-based UI with tabs: 'Users', 'Activity Log', 'Feedback', 'System'. (d) Users tab: list all users from dp_users (email, display_name, role, created_at, last_login), add user form (email, name, role), remove user button, change role dropdown. (e) Activity Log tab: show dp_activity_log entries (user email, action, details, timestamp) with filtering by user and date range. (f) Feedback tab: display all feedback from dp_feedback table. (g) System tab: show Supabase connection status, app version info, and quick stats (total users, active today, total datasets, etc).

Step 5 - Add user_id column awareness to existing database functions. In utils/database.py, modify save_dataset, save_report, save_rule_set, save_insight to include a user_id field (fetched from st.session_state.get('dp_user_id')) so data is separated per user. Modify list_* functions to filter by user_id when a user is logged in (admin sees all). This is a soft change - if dp_user_id is not in session_state, functions work as before (backward compatible).

Step 6 - Update SUPABASE_SETUP.md to add the complete SQL for: (a) dp_users table (id uuid PK, auth_id uuid references auth.users, email text unique, display_name text, role text default 'viewer', created_at timestamptz, last_login timestamptz, is_active boolean default true). (b) dp_activity_log table (id uuid PK, user_id uuid references dp_users, action text, details text, page text, created_at timestamptz). (c) Updated RLS policies that scope data to the authenticated user (dp_datasets, dp_reports, dp_insights, dp_validation_rule_sets get user_id column + policy). (d) Admin policies allowing admin role to see all rows. (e) A function or trigger to auto-create dp_users row on first Supabase Auth sign-up. (f) Index on dp_activity_log(user_id, created_at). Add all SQL in a new section called 'Authentication & User Management Tables'.
Step 7 - Update .streamlit/secrets.toml.example to add ADMIN_EMAIL field with comment explaining that the first user whose email matches this becomes admin automatically.

Step 8 - Add activity logging calls throughout the app. In app.py after successful auth, call log_user_activity('login'). In key pages (Upload, Data Cleaning, AI Insights, Report Generator), add a log_user_activity call when major actions are performed (dataset uploaded, cleaning applied, insight generated, report created). These calls should be non-blocking (wrapped in try/except).
acceptance_criteria: utils/auth.py exists with all listed functions and compiles without errors
app.py imports from utils/auth and calls require_auth() early in execution
When Supabase is NOT configured, the app works without login (local dev mode) - require_auth returns a mock user
When Supabase IS configured, users see a login page before accessing any content
pages/12_Admin_Panel.py exists with admin-only access check and user management UI
SUPABASE_SETUP.md contains complete SQL for dp_users and dp_activity_log tables with RLS policies
All new Python files pass py_compile without syntax errors
Existing pages continue to work - auth check is only in app.py (the entry point) and admin page
verification: python -c "from utils.auth import require_auth, get_current_user, sign_in, sign_out, sign_up, is_admin, log_user_activity, render_login_page; print('All auth imports OK')"
grep -q 'dp_users' SUPABASE_SETUP.md && grep -q 'dp_activity_log' SUPABASE_SETUP.md && echo 'SQL tables documented'
grep -q 'ADMIN_EMAIL' .streamlit/secrets.toml.example && echo 'Admin email in secrets template'
findings: All verification commands pass. py_compile succeeds on all modified files. Import test confirms all auth functions are importable. The auth gate in app.py uses local dev mode (mock admin user) when Supabase is not configured, ensuring zero breakage for existing users. Activity logging uses try/except to never block. The database.py user_id additions are fully backward compatible - functions work identically when dp_user_id is not in session state.
